items.title: Enhance OpenAIHK as Primary Router Channel in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates the OpenAIHK nano-banana worker as the primary channel in the failover router, enhancing reliability during Google API outages. Key changes include accepting the public model 'nano-banana-2' and updating the routing configuration to prioritize OpenAIHK, which uses the official API. This ensures improved performance and stability for users relying on the nano-banana service.

items.title: Enhance Connector E2E Testing with New CronJob in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an hourly Kubernetes CronJob named `auth-backend-connector-e2e`, which runs a new management command to verify the functionality of connectors through real product paths. Key features include checks for catalog integrity, end-to-end queries, and authenticated connections against official MCP servers. The implementation aims to ensure reliability and alert operators of any failures in the connector system.

items.title: Enable Guest Browsing with Deferred Login in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update allows unauthenticated visitors to browse service pages, models, and pricing without being immediately redirected to the login page. Guests will only be prompted to log in when they attempt to perform an operation, enhancing user experience and accessibility. Key changes include modifications to the global authentication flow and updates to multiple operation handlers to support this new behavior.

items.title: Sync Skill Docs with Latest API Updates in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update synchronizes the skill documentation with the latest OpenAPI changes, ensuring that endpoint and model references are current. Key improvements include refreshed AI Chat endpoints, updated Fish Audio API references, and new documentation for Kling and Veo video functionalities. These changes enhance clarity and usability for developers integrating with the AceDataCloud platform.

items.title: Introducing Feishu Connector Skill in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the Feishu (飞书) connector skill, enabling users to interact with the Feishu Open Platform REST API. Key functionalities include reading and writing documents, managing sheets, handling calendar events, and sending messages, all within the user's OAuth context. The skill is gated behind the connector going live and requires specific environment variables for token management.

items.title: Enhance Mobile CI with Dry-Run and Concurrency Guard in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a dry-run mode for safe testing of the mobile CI pipeline, allowing users to validate gate decisions without triggering actual builds. Additionally, a concurrency guard has been implemented to prevent overlapping releases, ensuring smoother deployments. The Android rollout mechanism has also been corrected for accurate staged releases, enhancing overall deployment reliability.

items.title: Enhanced Duration Input for Maestro in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: The Maestro configuration panel now features a numeric input for duration, allowing users to specify values from 1 to 600 seconds (up to 10 minutes). This change supports the new per-duration billing model and replaces the previous fixed dropdown options. Additionally, the help text in both English and Chinese has been updated to reflect this new input method and billing structure.

items.title: Revamped Maestro Pricing Model in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a per-duration pricing model for Maestro videos, allowing durations up to 10 minutes. The new formula replaces the previous flat rate structure, ensuring fairer billing based on actual video length. Key changes include updated API rules, documentation across multiple languages, and enhanced testing for billing safety.

items.title: Enhance Feishu Connector Token Mapping in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the mapping of the Feishu connector token to the environment variable `$FEISHU_TOKEN` in the aichat2 worker. This change ensures that the `acedatacloud/feishu` skill can access the user's Feishu `user_access_token` under a cleaner environment variable name, improving the integration process. The modification is minimal, consisting of a single line change in the codebase.

items.title: Introducing Feishu OAuth Connector in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've added a new Feishu (飞书) OAuth 2.0 connector to our catalog, filling a significant gap for the Chinese enterprise market. This connector integrates seamlessly with our existing `lark-*` tools and is designed to go live once the necessary credentials are set. Comprehensive tests have been implemented to ensure functionality and reliability.

items.title: Automated Daily Production Release for Mobile in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a fully automated daily production release workflow for both iOS and Android, triggered at 01:00 UTC. The process includes a review-state gate that ensures no submissions occur while apps are under review or if the version is not newer than the live version. Key safety mechanisms have been implemented to prevent issues during the release process.

items.title: Streamline Computer Actions with One-Click Pre-approval in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new one-click button to pre-approve all computer actions in the Settings under Local Tools. By enabling this feature, users can grant persistent permissions for all `computer.*` actions, streamlining the AI's ability to control the screen without repeated prompts. Additionally, it ensures that necessary macOS permissions are triggered as part of the process, enhancing user experience and security.

items.title: Enhance connector catalog resilience in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the connector catalog fetching mechanism by adding retries for connection-level errors and serving the last-known-good catalog when fetch attempts fail. These changes prevent users from receiving incorrect authorization prompts due to transient backend issues. Comprehensive tests have been added to ensure the reliability of the new behavior.

items.title: Enhance short-drama captions and runtime accuracy in Platform Service 🎬
items.summary: This update introduces two key improvements to the short-drama skill, enhancing the quality of captions and ensuring runtime accuracy. Captions will now be well-formed, addressing issues with run-together text, while a new runtime floor check ensures that clips meet the target length, preventing undershooting. These changes apply to new tasks following the redeployment of the worker image.

items.title: Enhance Service Introduction Guidelines in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'Two-File Convention' for service introduction content and design, ensuring consistency across documentation. Key changes include mandatory checks for rendering and formatting, as well as a correction in the file path for service introduction documents. These improvements aim to streamline the documentation process and enhance user experience.

items.title: Document Rendering Pitfalls Addressed in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the `INTRODUCTION_DESIGN.md` by adding a new section on rendering pitfalls, crucial for maintaining document integrity. Key changes include strict guidelines against empty lines, proper usage of HTML elements for FAQs, and recommendations for code examples. These adjustments aim to prevent rendering issues that could disrupt the user experience.

items.title: Fix markdown rendering issues and improve service intros in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses critical rendering issues in service introduction pages caused by markdown-it, specifically fixing HTML leaks and improving FAQ functionality. Additionally, it enhances the user experience by updating step indicators and cleaning up dead links to ensure all services are accurately represented. The changes impact 80 Chinese introduction pages and streamline the documentation process.

items.title: Enhance Consent Management for Computer Tools in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update restores persistent 'Always allow' consent for computer-use tools and ensures that 'Allow for session' functions correctly. Users will no longer face repeated prompts during screenshot and action loops, streamlining their experience. The changes include name-scoped grants for better management and a new button for persistent consent, enhancing usability while maintaining safety protocols.

items.title: Streamline Translation Process to Prevent OOM Crashes in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the OOM crash loop in the translation CronJob by modifying how stale targets are processed. Instead of loading all target rows into memory, the new implementation streams only the necessary lightweight data, significantly reducing memory usage from ~921 MB to 122 MB. This change ensures that the CronJob can run successfully and start clearing the backlog of translations.

items.title: Retire Sora MCP Tutorials and Clean Up Docs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes seven tutorial documents related to the now-decommissioned Sora MCP service, along with associated content from overview pages. Key changes include the deletion of outdated markdown files and the necessary adjustments to ensure no references to Sora remain in the documentation. The Sora API documentation remains untouched as it is already marked private.

items.title: Confirmed no-op for `claude-code` docs sync in Clis 🚫
items.summary: The review of the `claude-code` documentation updates confirmed that no CLI sync work is required for commands, parameters, or endpoint coverage. The findings indicate that there are no corresponding CLI packages or OpenAPI specs to sync within the repository, resulting in no changes to code, tests, or documentation.

items.title: Enhance Screenshot Action Loop for Desktop Control in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces Phase 4 of the computer use functionality, implementing a structured action loop that requires a screenshot before any action is taken. It ensures that actions are based on the most recent screen capture, enhancing operational hygiene and user control. Key changes include the addition of a new `<computer_use>` block and updates to the prompt builder and tests to support this functionality.

items.title: Introducing Global Panic-Stop Hotkey for Safety in Nexior 🚨
items.summary: This update implements a global 'panic stop' hotkey (Cmd/Ctrl + Alt + Shift + P) that instantly disables Computer Use, ensuring user safety during screen capture and automation. Key changes include the addition of a new helper function to manage the hotkey and updates to various components to reflect the state change in real-time. This enhancement reinforces the opt-in nature of Computer Use, keeping it default OFF while providing an immediate escape option.

items.title: Enhance Computer Input Functionality in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces Phase 2 of the computer use feature by adding nut.js as an optional dependency, enabling mouse and keyboard input functionality. The implementation ensures that the desktop app can now handle input commands like click, type, and scroll, while maintaining a CI-safe environment. Users can opt-in to this feature, which remains off by default, ensuring a smooth experience across platforms.

items.title: Updated Platform Table for Clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The platform table in the Claude Code documentation has been streamlined to accurately reflect the three published sub-documents: VS Code, Terminal, and GitHub Actions. The entry for Cursor has been removed, ensuring consistency with the actual content. This change only affects the Chinese source file and has been synchronized with the translation table.

items.title: Align MCP Documentation Slug and Title in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update renames the public documentation slug from `acedata-docs-mcp` to `acedatacloud-mcp`, ensuring consistency with the unified server naming. A new migration script has been added to handle the alias and title changes, while preserving old URLs to avoid 404 errors. Additionally, translations have been updated to reflect the new naming convention without losing existing content.

items.title: Enhance Screenshot Functionality in Nexior 📸
items.summary: This update introduces Phase 1 of the computer use feature, allowing the `computer.screenshot` tool to return a JPEG-encoded image of the screen. Key changes include resizing the image to logical resolution and ensuring coordinate mapping aligns with the model's expectations. Additionally, improvements were made to handle multi-monitor setups and optimize image quality to fit within data limits.

items.title: Enhance Client Tool with Image Support in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability for desktop client tool results to include images, allowing vision models to process visual inputs effectively. Key changes include the addition of an optional `image` field in tool results and improved validation to ensure only valid image URLs are accepted. This enhancement aims to close the perceive-act loop, improving the overall functionality of the platform.

items.title: Fix provider logos in dropdown for clarity in Backend 🎨
items.summary: This update corrects the **Providers** dropdown in the AI Model Directory to display the company logos instead of product logos. A new mapping has been implemented to ensure that each provider's row accurately reflects its company logo, enhancing user experience and clarity. Validation tests have been added to confirm the correct logos are displayed.

items.title: Removed Midjourney MCP Tutorials for Compliance in Backend 🚫
items.summary: This update removes seven Midjourney MCP tutorials due to a legal complaint, ensuring compliance with regulations. All related links have been stripped from various index pages, and the content has been cleared to prevent any access. The changes affect 114 files with a total of 8,440 deletions, maintaining the integrity of the documentation.

items.title: Fix store links and navigation issues in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves two major issues on the macOS desktop app's /download page. Store links now open externally, allowing users to access Google Play, the App Store, and GitHub without restrictions. Additionally, a Back/Forward navigation menu has been added, ensuring users can easily navigate away from the page without feeling trapped.

items.title: Revamped MCP Documentation for Unified Access in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The documentation for the MCP has been completely rewritten to reflect the new unified account management and directory/documentation MCP under `mcp-acedatacloud`. Key changes include updated URLs, authentication requirements, and tool prefixes. This ensures users have accurate and accessible information for utilizing the platform effectively.

items.title: Revamp AceKit Overview with Real Examples in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The AceKit overview documentation has been thoroughly rewritten to provide accurate and verified information. Key updates include the removal of hardcoded skill counts, the addition of live examples demonstrating real outputs, and a clarified distinction between MCP, CLI, and SDK offerings. This ensures users have a clear understanding of AceKit's capabilities and avoids previous inaccuracies.

items.title: Fix tool popup authorization for file paths in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update connects the desktop app's consent popup with the file system's allowlist, ensuring that approving the popup grants access to the specified file paths. Users no longer need to pre-add folders in Settings, as the approved paths are now authorized in-memory. Additionally, the changes include new tests to verify that consented paths are accessible, enhancing overall functionality and security.

items.title: Improve model card layout for better name visibility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update moves the modality pill from the header to the tags row in the model cards, preventing long model names from being truncated. The layout change ensures that the model name can utilize the full width of the card while consistently displaying the modality. No data or logic changes were made, only a layout adjustment for improved user experience.

items.title: Enhance Local Tools UI with Consistent Status and Solid Buttons in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the Local Tools section in the Nexior desktop user-settings dialog. It standardizes permission status labels to 'Granted' and 'Not granted' and updates action buttons to solid styles for better visibility. Additionally, the 'Open' button label has been simplified by removing the ellipsis, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance Model Cards with Official Price Labels in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the clarity of the `/models` cards by adding visible labels for the official price and a 'vs official' caption for the discount badge. The changes ensure that users can easily understand the pricing information without needing to hover for tooltips. This aligns the display with the existing detail page, enhancing user experience.

items.title: Optimize macOS Fullscreen Experience in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update removes the unnecessary inset for the chat-rail logo in fullscreen mode on macOS, enhancing the user interface. It tracks the fullscreen state and only applies the inset when not in fullscreen, ensuring a cleaner look. Key files modified include the Electron main and preload scripts, as well as the Navigator component.

items.title: Exciting New Desktop Tools for AI Control in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a proof-of-concept for desktop 'computer use', allowing the AI to capture screenshots and control the mouse and keyboard on the user's machine. Six new local tools have been added, which are opt-in and default to OFF, ensuring no unnecessary prompts for web or mobile users. The implementation includes robust consent mechanisms and internationalization support across 18 locales.

items.title: Enhance Kling CLI with New Lip-Sync and Talking-Photo Commands in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new commands for lip-sync and talking-photo functionalities in the Kling CLI, aligning it with the latest OpenAPI specifications. The new commands support various input modes and parameters, ensuring a seamless user experience while maintaining existing command behaviors. Additionally, validation for command parameters has been implemented to enhance reliability.

items.title: Q2 Roadmap Re-baseline Complete in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: The public roadmap has been successfully re-baselined at the end of Q2, confirming 100% delivery of Q2 tasks while rescheduling undelivered work to Q3. Key items delivered include the discount comparison feature and automated invoice generation. The total task count remains unchanged at 79, with adjustments made only to the phases array.

items.title: Remove face-swap probe to optimize resources in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `test-face-swap` end-to-end probe, which was consuming a significant amount of piapi quota with a high failure rate. The probe's deployment and execution scripts have been deleted to prevent unnecessary resource usage while maintaining the core face swap service and related tests. This change is expected to enhance overall system efficiency.

items.title: Docs sync confirms CLI alignment in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update verifies that the `flux`, `nano-banana`, and `seedream` CLIs are fully aligned with the latest OpenAPI specifications from AceDataCloud/Docs. A thorough comparison revealed no discrepancies in command surfaces, request options, or endpoint bindings, ensuring that no code changes are necessary at this time.

items.title: Fix theme switcher issue in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where the Settings theme switcher would incorrectly flip the theme upon opening. The fix involves mirroring the currently applied theme from the live HTML class, preventing any unintended changes during the mount phase. Unused methods related to cookie loading have also been removed for cleaner code.

items.title: Enhance WAV File Handling for Faster Responses in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update optimizes the `/suno/wav` endpoint by making the file storage process non-blocking. Users will now receive the upstream `file_url` immediately, while the background task handles the storage to COS without delaying the response. This change significantly reduces the risk of client timeouts and improves overall user experience.

items.title: New Desktop Installers for Windows and macOS in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces desktop installer cards for Windows and macOS on the public download page, enhancing accessibility for users. The installers link directly to the corresponding GitHub Release assets, ensuring easy access to the latest versions. Additionally, the update includes localization for 18 languages, making the download experience more user-friendly across different regions.

items.title: Enhance Settings Visibility for Web Only in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update restricts several operator and white-label settings tabs to the web surface, preventing their visibility in native iOS/Android and desktop applications. Key changes include the introduction of a new `isWebSurface` gate for tabs like Site, SEO, and Custom Domain, ensuring they are only accessible on the web. Comprehensive tests have been added to validate these changes and ensure robust functionality.

items.title: Streamline Model ID Display on Cards in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update addresses the duplication of the native model ID on the model cards and detail pages. Now, the ID will only display when it differs from the name, enhancing clarity and improving SEO descriptions. Key files modified include List.vue and Detail.vue, ensuring a cleaner presentation without losing any functionality.

items.title: Q2 Roadmap Re-baseline Complete! 🎉 in Roadmap
items.summary: The public roadmap has been successfully re-baselined at the end of Q2, confirming 100% delivery of Q2 tasks. Key items such as the automated invoice generation and advanced routing have been completed and moved into the Q2 group. Q3 now includes rescheduled tasks without any loss in total task count, ensuring a clear path forward for upcoming deliverables.

items.title: Updated Video API Documentation with Latest Models in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update enhances the video API documentation by replacing outdated model examples with the latest versions for Seedance, Kling, and Veo. All response examples have been updated to reflect real validated outputs, ensuring accuracy and relevance. The changes improve the user experience by providing current and functional examples for developers.

items.title: Updated Chat Model Examples for Clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update refreshes the main example models across 10 chat documentation files to the latest versions, ensuring they are live-validated and functional. Key changes include upgrades from older models like `gpt-5.4` to `gpt-5.5`, and similar updates for other models, enhancing the accuracy and usability of the documentation. All changes have been tested and verified on the live API.

items.title: Update image models and fix documentation errors in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the image API documentation by replacing outdated model references with the latest validated models. Key changes include correcting model strings in the seedream documentation, upgrading the flux model, and incorporating the flagship model for nanobanana images. All modifications have been verified against live API responses to ensure accuracy and functionality.

items.title: Renamed 'music' Modality to 'audio' in Frontend Web 🎶
items.summary: This update refactors the modality naming in the models directory, changing 'music' to 'audio' for better alignment with the backend catalog. Key files updated include List.vue and Detail.vue, along with localization files across 12 languages. This change enhances clarity for users interacting with audio-related services.

items.title: Refactor audio modality naming for clarity in Backend 🎶
items.summary: This update renames the 'music' modality to 'audio' in the catalog, aligning it with the correct service category. Changes were made in the catalog utility and test files to reflect this new naming convention. All tests passed successfully, ensuring the integrity of the functionality.

items.title: Enhance Balance Card Experience in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the balance card on the document details page by vertically centering the App ID and restructuring the balance explanations for clarity. The layout has been changed to a vertical arrangement, and unit suffixes have been added to the balance values. Additionally, a visual indicator for the selected balance type has been implemented, enhancing user interaction.

items.title: Boost MongoDB Performance with New Indexes in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces critical indexes for the MongoDB collections, significantly reducing full-collection scans (COLLSCAN) that were causing high memory usage. The changes ensure that both the failover-router and persona update processes utilize these indexes, leading to a drastic decrease in documents scanned per second and improved cache performance. The implementation is designed to be self-healing, preventing future regressions in database performance.

items.title: OpenAI CLI Sync Confirmed in Clis 🚀
items.summary: The OpenAI CLI has been reviewed and confirmed to be in sync with the latest upstream Docs OpenAPI updates. Key areas such as endpoint coverage and command exposure were assessed, revealing that no changes are necessary as the CLI already supports all current API paths and model variants. This PR serves as a confirmation of the alignment with the recent documentation.

items.title: Enhance Providers Dropdown with Brand Icons in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces brand favicons for each provider in the Providers dropdown of the public AI Model Directory. The implementation includes a new `ICatalogProvider` interface and updates to the `List.vue` component to display icons alongside provider names, improving the visual appeal and user experience. Fallbacks are in place for missing logos, ensuring a seamless display.

items.title: Enhance Provider Directory with Icons in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `providers` array to the `/models/catalog/` response, providing a deduplicated list of providers along with their brand icons and model counts. The implementation ensures that the UI can display icons next to each provider seamlessly. Validation tests confirm the functionality and integrity of the changes.

items.title: Auto-translate i18n updates in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated translation updates for various languages based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications include the addition of new keys and translations across multiple language files, enhancing the internationalization support of the platform. This will improve accessibility for users across different regions.

items.title: Revamp Admin Console with Solid Buttons and Enhanced Detection History in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the admin LLM upstream control-plane page by introducing solid action buttons for improved visibility and usability. The footer layout has been adjusted to prevent cutoff issues, while the detection history drawer now features a richer display of event metrics. Additionally, five new internationalization keys have been added for better localization support.

items.title: Fix per-model pricing and model-id names in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical pricing issue in the public model directory by implementing per-model pricing, ensuring each model displays its correct cost instead of a uniform service-wide price. Additionally, model identifiers are now displayed as their raw IDs across the platform, enhancing clarity. The changes improve the accuracy of pricing information and streamline the backend processes without requiring frontend modifications.

items.title: Merged OpenAI Chat Completion Docs for Clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the OpenAI Chat Completion documentation by merging the standalone gpt-4o-image examples into the main guide, enhancing clarity and accuracy. The outdated standalone document has been retired, and its content is now part of the primary documentation, ensuring users have access to the most current information. Additionally, necessary redirects have been implemented to maintain SEO integrity.

items.title: Enhance Tool Selection for Local File Access in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the tool selection process for accessing local files in the desktop app. The Bash tool's description has been clarified to indicate it does not access the user's machine, while new prompts ensure that local tools are prioritized for user-specific queries. These changes enhance user experience by providing accurate responses regarding local file systems.

items.title: Enhance Model Cards with Official Pricing in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the official provider price display on the `/models` cards, enhancing clarity on savings. Each card now shows the official price as a struck-through figure, along with a tooltip for the savings percentage. This change ensures users can easily understand the value proposition at a glance.

items.title: Enhance Logo Visibility for Dark Mode in Frontend Web 🌙
items.summary: This update modifies the logo rendering on the official AceData site to ensure the dark-aware wordmark is displayed in dark mode. The change bypasses the tenant logo for the official site, allowing the built-in wordmark to adapt to the dark theme while preserving custom logos for white-label tenants. Verification confirms that the logo is now visible in dark mode without affecting other configurations.

items.title: Updated API Documentation with Live-Validated Examples in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new plans research document that audits and refreshes the example models used in the `development_*.md` API documentation. Key findings include the identification of outdated model references and the implementation of real-time API requests to validate the latest models, ensuring accurate and reliable documentation for users. The changes enhance the usability and correctness of the API examples, providing a solid foundation for future updates.

items.title: Fix task type for nano-banana to ensure studio visibility in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the task type from 'image' to 'images' in the nano-banana failover-router configuration. This change ensures that tasks submitted to the studio are now properly listed, resolving a visibility issue caused by a label mismatch. The fix has been applied to the live environment and verified to restore functionality.

items.title: Introducing Fish CLI for Audio TTS in Clis 🎤
items.summary: This update adds a new Fish CLI package that implements all four endpoints from the Fish Audio TTS API. Users can now generate text-to-speech audio, manage voice models, and query task statuses directly from the command line. The architecture aligns with existing CLIs, ensuring a seamless experience for developers.

items.title: Enhance Local Tools Settings with Persistent Consent in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a dedicated 'Local Tools' tab in the Settings dialog for desktop users, improving accessibility to local tool configurations. Additionally, a new 'Always allow' consent option has been implemented, allowing users to grant persistent permissions for specific tools, enhancing user control and experience. These changes streamline the management of local tools and permissions, ensuring a more intuitive interface.

items.title: Streamlined Audio for Short Dramas in Platform Service 🎬
items.summary: This update transitions short dramas to version 1.8 by eliminating any TTS voice, including narration, from the final cut. All dialogue will now be generated from on-screen characters using r2v, ensuring a more authentic audio experience. Key modifications include the removal of the narrator entry from the voices.json and adjustments to subtitle synchronization for accuracy.

items.title: Fix local tool input handling and path expansion in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses critical issues in the desktop app where local tool calls were failing due to improper handling of Vue reactive Proxies and unexpanded home directory paths. Key changes include deep-copying the tool input before IPC calls and implementing a new function to expand '~' in file paths, ensuring smoother execution and accurate directory resolution. The fixes have been verified with successful builds and functional tests on macOS.

items.title: Cleanup of Orphaned Docs and Articles in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes 26 private and orphaned documents along with 20 orphaned promotional articles from the documentation. The changes include a migration script that deletes outdated content and repoints links in surviving documents to ensure a clean and navigable documentation structure. This cleanup enhances the overall quality and relevance of the documentation for users.

items.title: Fix Seedream Pricing Display in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update restores the correct pricing display for Seedream models in Studio by fixing the cost rules to use the appropriate dotted format. Previously, the price panel showed zero for certain models due to a misconfiguration in the cost rules. The changes ensure that the display now accurately reflects the billed amounts, enhancing user experience and clarity.
